split_plan.child_description: DESIGN.md format spec, templates, and authoring rules. rationale: The body is already 4999 tokens / 153 lines — at the cap before embedding a single TOC — and the embed contract (one `> [file] Title · Title · …` line per referenced file, embedding its complete TOC) costs ~3840 tokens across the 31 distinct referenced files (553 headings, ~3516 title tokens + separators/overhead), so any single-SKILL form lands near 5340-5640 tokens; prose-trim cannot close that gap because moving sections out only adds more embeds and the irreducible operational core (triggers + 9 operations + hard rules + instructions) still cannot share a body with 3840 tokens of embeds. The skill is genuinely multi-domain (spec, authoring, extraction, audit, convert/apply) and the 31 references partition cleanly into those 5 domains with small per-child embed burdens (440-1186 tokens), so each child's lean operational body (~1400-1600 tokens) plus its embeds totals 1840-2786 tokens — comfortably under the 4800 headroom and 500-line limits — with the 4 cross-skill refs shared so each child embeds only the 1-2 it actually links.
